user_message: Quick one -- build the narrative on Wide World Importers. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 09:58 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers escalation triage

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 14:24 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 12:16 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-03-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Priya (Wide World Importers)

[00:01:15] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:05:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:07:00] Priya (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:20:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
